title: Colly Collectors, Callback Order, Limits, Storage, and Adoption Boundaries · Go Build Room
summary: A cooperative of small public-service publishers incrementally builds policy-preserving Colly adapter and decision record from original deterministic HTTP, robots, sitemap, HTML, graph, and report fixtures while retaining prior Go, web, Git, accessibility, security, testing, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of policy-preserving Colly adapter and decision record while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explici…
objectives: Map Collector creation, callback registration, Visit, Request, Async, Wait, error, and close-related ownership into the application lifecycle.
Translate allowed domains, depth, body size, redirects, robots, rate limits, revisit, and user agent from owned policy and verify gaps.
Keep callbacks thin, order-independent where required, concurrency-safe, context-rich, and delegated to testable application services.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Colly Lifecycle
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Go team handles case 6264 during a mixed-origin fairness and race rehearsal. Build deterministic Go evidence to map Colly lifecycle, policy, callbacks, storage, and differentials without losing owned invariants; browser Go remains deterministic and free of native network, parser, filesystem, process, and host effects; then change one content version, metadata, accessibility, resource, browser-service, or report condition without overclaiming. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, x/net/html, goquery, Colly, files, processes, race, load, assistive-technology review, containers, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Map Collector creation, callback registration, Visit, Request, Async, Wait, error, and close-related ownership into the application lifecycle.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Colly Lifecycle.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for map collector creation, callback registration, visit, request, async, wait, error, and close-related ownership into the application lifecycle. For Authorized site auditing and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Colly Lifecycle, keep Colly Lifecycle connected to executable Go authorization, URL, robots, response, tree, frontier, goroutine, report, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.
